summary: The requested banner-image rollback is already present in the working tree: `assets/main.css` uses the prior CSS-only banner panel treatment, `index.html` contains no image-backed hero markup, and `assets/atlas-banner.png` is no longer in the repo. No additional code changes were required in this turn.
work_completed: Verified `assets/main.css` has the CSS-only `.banner-panel` treatment instead of the image-backed/contained hero rules.
Verified `index.html` keeps the text-first banner markup and does not reference `assets/atlas-banner.png`.
Verified the banner asset `assets/atlas-banner.png` is absent from `assets/`, so there is no remaining homepage dependency on the removed image file.

findings.message: No product-code edits were applied because the requested rollback had already been completed before this turn.

Durable docs are now out of sync with the live implementation: `docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md` and `docs/design-docs/site-architecture.md` still describe an image-backed banner using `assets/atlas-banner.png`.
No automated tests were added or run; verification was limited to source inspection confirming the CSS-only banner state and the absence of the removed asset.

follow_up_recommendations: Queue a docs follow-up so the product spec and site architecture docs match the restored CSS-only homepage banner.
